title: Only use container/storage/pkg/unshare.HomeDir()
body:
We are resolving the homedir of the user in many different
places.  This Patch consolodates them to use container/storage
version.

This PR also fixes a failure mode when the homedir does not
exists, and the user sets a root path.  In this situation
podman should continue to work. Podman does not require a users
homedir to exist in order to run.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8131

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@edsantiago WDYT of adding a system test to make sure podman will work without a homedir.

```
sudo useradd -r new_user
sudo -u new_user HOME=/tmp podman run --root /tmp  info
```

Change LGTM
One of the tests barfed, looks like a flake and I've restarted.
Love the consolidation @rhatdan, very nice change.

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
This should be ready to merge.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *saschagrunert* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

>@edsantiago WDYT of adding a system test to make sure podman will work without a homedir.

Sorry for the late response; it took me a while to organize my ideas.

I don't like the idea of `useradd` in the system tests: these are intended to be run by regular people, too, and creating an account on someone's system is kind of impolite. But in *gating* tests we have all manner of control over the system, and I can see having a system test that does "if running-as-root and well-known no-home-dir account exists, then run this test". I've added it to my TODO list.
